channel.title: SpotPulse Intelligence Trending
channel.description: World's First Autonomous Global Trend Intelligence Engine.

item.title: Health Officials Downplay Pandemic Risk From Cruise Hantavirus Outbreak
item.description: The deadly hantavirus outbreak on a cruise ship doesn’t risk triggering the next pandemic, World Health Organization officials said, downplaying an incident that has raised concern about a new viral c

item.title: Russia and Ukraine trade accusations of truce violations
item.description: Russia and Ukraine on Saturday accused the other of violating a a three-day US-brokered ceasefire, which coincides with Russia's World War II victory celebrations. No major strikes were reported by th

item.title: U.S. strikes two Iranian-flagged tankers as tensions continue amid ceasefire
item.description: The Trump administration said it was anticipating a reply from Tehran on its latest terms for ending the war. Iran said the U.S. proposal is still under review.

item.title: The Iran war will change global energy markets in these important ways, oil executives say
item.description: Iran's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz has resulted in the loss of nearly a billion barrels of oil with the shortage growing worse every day.

item.title: Scientists say next few years vital to securing the future of the West Antarctic Ice Sheet
item.description: Collapse of the West Antarctic Ice Sheet could be triggered with very little ocean warming above present-day, leading to a devastating four meters of global sea level rise to play out over hundreds of

item.title: Australia's right-wing One Nation party scores historic parliamentary win
item.description: One Nation gains its first seat in the lower house, in a by-election seen as a key test for the populist party.

item.title: Using AI to expand global access to reliable flood forecasts
item.description:
Posted by Yossi Matias, VP Engineering & Research, and Grey Nearing, Research Scientist, Google Research
Floods are the most common natural disaster , and are responsible for roughly $50 billio

item.title: Radioactive rain and proving relativity: Books in brief
item.description: Nature, Published online: 08 May 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-01500-0 Andrew Robinson reviews five of the best science picks.

item.title: Being in nature can help people with chronic back pain manage their condition
item.description: Researchers asked patients, some of whom had experienced lower back pain for up to 40 years, if being in nature helped them coped better with their lower back pain. They found that people able to spen

item.title: "China is Just Watching": Ro Khanna on Why the Iran Conflict is a Gift to Beijing
item.description: With 80,000 manufacturing jobs lost, Rep. Ro Khanna (D-CA) argues that the US needs a tougher, more strategic approach to China. He discusses the impact of unfair trade practices on American ports and

item.title: Trump's feuds and tensions with allies likely to outlast Iran war
item.description: Allies from Europe to the Middle East to the Indo-Pacific are once again fearful that the U.S. might be unreliable in a future crisis.

item.title: Surge in fake citations uncovered by audit of 2.5 million biomedical science papers
item.description: Nature, Published online: 08 May 2026; doi:10.1038/d41586-026-00748-w An analysis of 97 million citations has found that rates of fabricated citations have climbed steeply since 2023.
